{{Quote|If ''I'' can't have the materials from Vandelhelm... they're ''not'' going to end up refitting the Alliance fleet!|Mordur|<ref name="marvel98" />}}
'''Mordur''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]—after the Empire's climactic defeat in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]—Mordur was involved in a plot to undermine the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]' control of the key shipbuilding world of [[Vandelhelm/Legends|Vandelhelm]]. Working out of a converted [[Drone barge/Legends|drone barge]], Mordur conspired with [[Metalsmiths Guild]] [[Guild Master/Legends|Guildmaster]] [[Orrk]] to eliminate the "Venerated Ones," two child royals, thus allowing them to take full control of Vandelhelm's metal exports. Their plan was foiled by the slick [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] of Alliance agent [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who [[Mission to Vandelhelm|brought]] the Venerated Ones safely to Vandelhelm, [[Death/Legends|killing]] Orrk after exposing his treachery. In response, Mordur set Vandelhelm's fleet of drone barges to fly into the [[Star system/Legends|system]]'s [[Star/Legends|star]], but Solo prevented this plot as well.<ref name="marvel98" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mordur appeared as an antagonist in [[Star Wars (1977) 98|''Star Wars'' (1977) 98]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] and released in [[1985]].<ref name="marvel98" />
